description: Charlie Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, reportedly expressed deep anger toward Candace Owens over her public claims surrounding Kirk's 2025 assassination. Family sources say Erika wants justice, not revenge, as the murder case against Tyler Robinson moves toward a possible trial. Owens defended her stance, saying unanswered questions about Kirk's death remain.

title: 'They will pay many times over': Trump as US carries out 10th night of Iran strikes

Iran also targeted US allies Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain with missile barrages.  Diplomatic efforts continue while the interim ceasefire agreement has collapsed.  Global energy supplies face fresh concerns as oil prices climb.

The Odyssey delivered Christopher Nolan's biggest opening day in India.

title: Defence ministry seeks firms for Rs 15,000 cr high-altitude airship project

The program aims to offer lower-cost alternatives to existing satellite systems.  Airships will hover in the stratosphere, bridging gaps between drones and satellites.  The Indian Air Force is actively partnering with local aerospace companies for this initiative.

Burnham met the king at Buckingham Palace before heading to Downing Street. He promised to address the cost of living and build more council homes. The new prime minister aims to create a new political economic model.

author: TOI ENTERTAINMENT DESK
title: 'Ramayana' trailer reviews: Ranbir starrer hailed as 'SPECTACULAR'
description: Filmmakers and industry insiders have shared overwhelmingly positive early reactions to the 'Ramayana' trailer. They praised the film's grand scale, visual effects, and performances by the cast. Ranbir Kapoor as Lord Rama and Yash as Ravana received specific commendations for their roles. The trailer is set to be unveiled publicly on July 24. This cinematic adaptation of the Indian epic is slated for a Diwali 2026 worldwide release.

author: ATUL MATHUR
title: No decision on ethanol blending beyond 20%: Govt
description: The government has stated no decision exists to raise ethanol blending beyond twenty percent.  Lower-blended fuel or pure petrol will not be available at select outlets.  Any future diesel blending will depend on evaluations and stakeholder consultations.  The transition to E20 has been phased and evidence-based, with gradual increases.  Claims of vehicle damage from E20 are largely misleading or unsupported by technical evidence.

author: MANASH PRATIM GOHAIN
title: NTA rebuts claims of 5, says NEET-UG OMR sheets forged
description: The National Testing Agency has refuted claims of OMR answer sheet discrepancies from five NEET-UG candidates.  Images circulated by these candidates were reportedly digitally altered or regenerated using artificial intelligence.  The agency verified the original OMR sheets and scores for all five students involved.  These actions follow allegations of swapped OMR sheets and fewer attempted answers than recorded.  Candidates submitting fabricated records may face severe penalties under relevant acts.

author: KUMAR RAKESH
title: Youth full of aspirations, want country to move forward: PM Modi
description: Prime Minister Modi urged opposition parties to use logic and facts in Parliament.  He highlighted India's youth aspirations and recent national achievements.  The successful launch of a private space rocket showcased young Indian capabilities.  Recent inaugurations and a hydrogen train launch also marked progress.  India maintained growth despite global energy supply disruptions.

author: TOI WORLD DESK
title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, cites trade disputes
description: President Trump imposed fifty percent tariffs on most Canadian goods. This action escalates trade tensions between the two nations. The tariffs apply to many goods previously protected under the USMCA agreement. This move heightens economic uncertainty and strains bilateral ties. Aides are examining the possibility of imposing additional tariffs on Canada.

author: SUBODH GHILDIYAL
title: Rahul Gandhi claims PM Modi ‘most anti-youth PM India has seen’
description: Opposition leaders criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi for his government's actions.  They condemned the crackdown on young protesters marching towards Parliament.  Rahul Gandhi called Modi the most anti-youth Prime Minister India has seen.  Leaders stated that students are not criminals and their demands are legitimate.  The protests highlight widespread discontent regarding examination irregularities and education system issues.

author: TOI LIFESTYLE DESK
title: Maria Goretti says she misses financial independence after quitting MTV career for motherhood
description: Former MTV VJ Maria Goretti has opened up about stepping away from a successful television career after marrying actor Arshad Warsi, admitting that while she sometimes misses earning her own money and the appreciation that came with her work, she has no regrets about choosing motherhood. Maria also spoke about witnessing the ups and downs of husband Arshad Warsi's Bollywood journey, saying those experiences helped keep them grounded.

author: TNN
title: Day 1 of Parliament’s monsoon session washed out amid NEET, donation chori protests
description: The first day of Parliament's monsoon session was disrupted by opposition protests.  Opposition parties raised concerns over various issues, including temple donations and exam leaks.  Frequent adjournments led to the cancellation of proceedings in both houses.  A bill to increase the number of Supreme Court judges was introduced in Lok Sabha.  The changed seating arrangement for rebel TMC MPs also drew attention.

title: Rain continues to play havoc in Jammu, toll goes up to 28
